summary: Frontend-only retest of the useAuthRouting fix in /app/frontend/src/auth/AuthContext.tsx. All 5 targeted flows PASS on mobile viewport 390x844 against preview URL. (T1) Pending student 'pending/pending123' lands on pending-title; tapping pending-logout signs out and returns to Welcome (role cards visible, pending-title gone). (T2) Blocked student 'blocked/blocked123' lands on blocked-title; tapping blocked-logout returns to Welcome. (T3) Approved student 'student/student123' Settings -> student-settings-logout returns to Welcome. (T4) Admin 'admin/admin123' (Demo Hostel) Settings -> admin-settings-logout returns to Welcome. (T5) Regression: after logging out from pending screen, logging back in as approved student cleanly routes into (student) tabs (Today's Plan visible, bottom tabs Today/Menu/Wastage/Settings visible, no stuck nav). Code review of the fix: useAuthRouting correctly handles `path === '(auth)/pending' || path === '(auth)/blocked'` along with existing (student)/(admin) gating, and lastTarget ref guard prevents redirect loops. Only pre-existing cosmetic console warnings observed: 'shadow*' deprecated (theme.ts) and 'props.pointerEvents' deprecated — both noted in iteration_1 and non-blocking. Bug fix is verified.

issues: Pre-existing cosmetic console warnings carried over from iteration_1: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow"' (theme.ts shadow.card) and 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated' (RN web / safe-area-context). Non-blocking.

action_items: No fixes required. Bug 'sign out on pending/blocked screens does nothing' is RESOLVED.
(Optional polish, carried over) Replace shadow.* with boxShadow in theme.ts for web parity.
(Optional polish, carried over) Audit pointerEvents prop usage.
critical_code_review_comments: useAuthRouting now checks `segments.join('/')` against '(auth)/pending' and '(auth)/blocked' when user is null, in addition to the existing (student)/(admin) top-segment check. Correct and minimal fix.
lastTarget ref still prevents redirect loops; no regression observed across rapid logout->login cycles (T5).
Pending and Blocked screens both call useAuth().logout which clears token, user, and storage atomically. Routing then triggers replace('/').
All sign-out controls retain stable testIDs: pending-logout, blocked-logout, student-settings-logout, admin-settings-logout.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Web preview renders each testID twice due to Expo Router SSR + CSR. `.first` may resolve to the hidden SSR copy and report not-visible even though the CSR copy IS visible. Reliable pattern: query all matches via page.evaluate and filter by getBoundingClientRect()+computed style, or use `.last` for clicks. Used in iteration_2 helper visible_count().
rca_of_issue: Original bug: Sign-out buttons on /(auth)/pending and /(auth)/blocked invoked logout() (clearing token/user) but useAuthRouting only redirected away when top segment was '(student)' or '(admin)'. Since pending/blocked sit under (auth), null-user state did not trigger any router.replace, leaving the user stranded on the pending/blocked screen with no auth. Fix: extend the null-user branch to also redirect when full path is '(auth)/pending' or '(auth)/blocked'. Verified end-to-end via Playwright on the live preview URL.
